message.content: {"items":[{"title":"GESIS Panel, Standard Edition (Version 55.0.0)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14483","justification":"Long-running German probability-based online panel including waves in the 2020s with modules on political attitudes, political participation, trust in institutions and government; nationally representative for Germany."},{"title":"Politbarometer West 2020","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13659","justification":"Monthly survey series for Germany covering 2020; includes vote intention, party identification, evaluations of government and political actors, and ideological self-placement."},{"title":"Politbarometer 2021","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13844","justification":"Continuation of the German Politbarometer series in 2021 with detailed measures on political behaviour, attitudes toward government and parties, and left-right ideology."},{"title":"Politbarometer 2022","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14114","justification":"German Politbarometer year 2022; tracks political preferences, satisfaction with federal government and institutions, and ideological orientations in Germany."},{"title":"Politbarometer 2023","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14439","justification":"Politbarometer data for Germany in 2023 with rich indicators of political behaviour, party support, evaluations of political system performance, and ideological self-placement."},{"title":"German Longitudinal Election Study (GLES) 2021, Rolling Cross-Section Campaign Panel","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14108","justification":"GLES 2021 component focusing on the federal election campaign in Germany; includes vote choice, political participation, attitudes toward government and democracy, and ideological positions."},{"title":"German Longitudinal Election Study (GLES) 2021, Post-Election Cross-Section","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14109","justification":"Representative post-election survey for Germany after the 2021 federal election capturing political behaviour, party choice, satisfaction with democracy and government, and ideological self-location."},{"title":"German Longitudinal Election Study (GLES) 2021, Campaign Panel","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14077","justification":"Panel survey following German voters during the 2021 federal election campaign; measures political interest, evaluations of parties and government, and ideological orientations."},{"title":"German Longitudinal Election Study (GLES) 2021, Short-Term Campaign Panel","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14110","justification":"Short-term panel in Germany around the 2021 federal election examining campaign effects on political attitudes, participation intentions, and ideological positions."},{"title":"German Internet Panel (GIP), Waves 2020–2022 (GIP Welle 36-48)","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA5665","justification":"Probability-based online panel for Germany with waves in the early 2020s containing repeated measures of political attitudes, trust in government and institutions, and left-right ideology."}]}
